100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 28, 1926, the British general strike entered its third week, with workers across the nation continuing to demand better wages and working conditions, causing significant disruptions to industry and transport. In the United States, tensions rose as the economy showed signs of instability, prompting debates about the future of labor rights. Additionally, in Germany, the political climate remained volatile as the Weimar Republic grappled with economic challenges and rising extremist movements.
